summary: Frontend-only testing of SEO meta tags, JSON-LD structured data, robots.txt, sitemap.xml, and the ExitIntentPopup lead-capture flow for Coach Lady Wassa. 10/11 requested items PASS. One MINOR issue: duplicate og:* meta tags on blog pages because index.html ships static OG tags that Helmet does not manage, so blog article renders two <meta property="og:type">: first 'website' (static), second 'article' (Helmet). Most crawlers will pick the first, so dynamic og:type is effectively overridden. Title, description and Article JSON-LD all update correctly per article.

integration_issues.issue: Two <meta property="og:type"> tags present on /blog/:slug pages — first ('website') from /public/index.html, second ('article') from react-helmet-async. querySelector returns 'website'. Same duplication exists for og:title, og:description, og:image, og:url, og:locale, meta[name=description], meta[name=keywords], and <title>. Helmet injects new tags instead of replacing the static ones because they lack Helmet's tracking attribute. Impact: social share previews (FB/LinkedIn) that read the FIRST meta tag will always show the home-page default text even for blog articles.

seo_verification.home_title_value: Coach Lady Wassa Traoré | Cabinet Mindset Coaching — Neurosciences Appliquées
seo_verification.blog_title_example: 7 techniques neurosciences pour gérer le stress sans médicaments | Coach Lady Wassa
seo_verification.robots_txt_note: Cloudflare managed content signals are prepended automatically by the hosting layer; our custom rules (Allow /, Disallow /admin|/membre|/connexion|/inscription, Sitemap URL) follow correctly.

exit_intent_popup_verification.desktop_mouseleave_trigger: PASS — popup shows when mouseleave dispatched with clientY<=0
exit_intent_popup_verification.close_X_button: PASS — dismisses popup and sets localStorage lw_exit_popup_seen_v1
exit_intent_popup_verification.close_backdrop_click: PASS — backdrop click dismisses and sets localStorage flag
exit_intent_popup_verification.seven_day_suppression: PASS — after dismiss + reload, mouseleave no longer re-opens popup
exit_intent_popup_verification.mobile_no_blocking: PASS — on 390x844 viewport popup does NOT show immediately; 45s timer fallback is used; page fully interactive

admin_regression.create_member_visible: PASS — 'Créer un membre' button present on Membres tab
admin_regression.existing_members_loaded: PASS — 7 members listed (including TEST_/uiuser_/pytest_ leftovers from prior iterations)

action_items: Remove duplicate SEO tags from /app/frontend/public/index.html (og:type, og:title, og:description, og:image, og:url, og:locale, <title>, meta[name=description], meta[name=keywords]). Let react-helmet-async be the sole source. OR alternatively, set <Helmet><meta ...></Helmet> wrapper so Helmet can find and replace them (Helmet replaces meta tags only if their attribute signature matches one it has seen before — adding data-react-helmet="true" to each static tag in index.html also works). Without this fix, social share previews (Facebook/LinkedIn/Twitter) for blog articles will show the home-page defaults instead of per-article OG data.
(Low priority, pre-existing) Clean up TEST-prefixed member records from earlier test iterations (UI Test User, Enr, RS, Reset User, Pytest User) in the members collection.
critical_code_review_comments: ExitIntentPopup.js: on axios failure the code silently marks the popup as 'submitted' and stores the localStorage flag — the user sees a success screen even though the lead was never saved. Consider at least logging server error and perhaps retrying once before giving up. Also no client-side email format validation beyond type=email.

ExitIntentPopup.js: 45-second mobile fallback is very long and not user-configurable. Also relies on window.innerWidth at fire time — if user rotates device between load and timer, trigger may not fire.
SEO.js: 'areaServed: [BF, CI, SN, FR, CA, BE]' and priceRange '€€' are placeholder values — confirm with business before production.
public/sitemap.xml is a static file: new blog articles added via admin will NOT automatically appear. Consider generating /api/sitemap.xml on the backend or documenting the manual update step.

robots.txt lists Sitemap URL for production domain (coachladywassa.com) — correct for prod, but means sitemap on preview URL will not be auto-discovered by crawlers on the preview env. This is intentional and expected.

success_rate.frontend: ~91% (10/11 items fully pass; 1 minor og:type duplication issue)

seed_data_creation: None — all tests use ephemeral data. One real lead created in /api/leads with email testuser_exit@example.com, profile_type=exit_intent, id=43b5337d-8ba8-495a-acdb-53db2f647c9d.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All SEO + popup testids are stable. Popup localStorage key is 'lw_exit_popup_seen_v1' — clear it before each run. Desktop trigger is reliably simulated via JS-dispatched MouseEvent('mouseleave', {clientY:-5}) on document. Blog article slug used for testing: gerer-stress-neurosciences (exists in seed). Main action item for E1: de-dupe the SEO meta tags between /public/index.html and /components/SEO.js. After that fix, re-verify og:type='article' on blog pages.
rca_of_issue: Duplicate og:type root cause: /app/frontend/public/index.html includes a full set of static SEO meta tags (lines with og:type, og:title, og:description, og:image, og:url, og:locale, plus <title>, description, keywords). react-helmet-async only replaces tags it has previously rendered — the pre-existing static tags in index.html are INVISIBLE to Helmet, so Helmet appends its own tags to <head> instead of replacing the static ones. Result: two of each meta on every page. querySelector (and most crawlers) reads the FIRST match, which is the static one → blog pages always expose og:type=website, og:title=home default, etc. Fix: either (a) remove the static OG/description/keywords/title tags from index.html and keep only <title> as a loading placeholder, or (b) add attribute data-react-helmet="true" to each static tag so Helmet will overwrite them. Option (a) is cleaner and recommended; the trade-off is that non-JS crawlers (FB/LinkedIn scrapers DO execute JS nowadays, but some still don't) will not see any OG tags. If SSR/prerender is not planned, option (b) is the safer compromise.
